name: Olympus Financial
description_short: Correspondent mortgage lender licensed in 5 states offering conventional, FHA, VA, jumbo, non-QM, and investment property loans with 80+ investor relationships and 24/7 technology support.
description_long:
Olympus Financial is a correspondent mortgage lender established in 2023, operating as a direct lender rather than a traditional broker. The company is licensed in Florida, Colorado, Georgia, Texas, and Illinois (NMLS #2469800) and positions itself as a modernized alternative to traditional mortgage origination, with emphasis on speed, technology, and handling non-traditional borrower scenarios.

The company offers a comprehensive suite of mortgage products including conventional conforming loans, FHA and VA mortgages, jumbo loans above conforming limits, non-QM and alternative documentation programs (bank statement, asset depletion, ITIN, no-doc), DSCR investment loans, and specialty programs for foreign nationals and non-warrantable condos. Additional services include fix-and-flip financing, BRRR strategies, HELOCs, down payment assistance, and helolans. Their marketing emphasizes ability to service self-employed, freelance, and real estate investor borrowers who may not qualify under traditional guidelines.

Olympus distinguishes itself through correspondent lending capability (lending directly rather than brokering), access to 80+ wholesale and correspondent investors, proprietary technology enabling 24/7 loan officer availability, and a stated philosophy of "no unnecessary layers" with no middle management between borrowers and pricing. The company highlights flat organizational structure and loan officer-built infrastructure. Google reviews (5.0 stars, 34 reviews) cite fast closings (under 30 days), handling of complex borrower situations, and personalized service from loan officers.

Limitations include geographic licensing to only 5 states, making them unavailable nationally. The company is relatively new (2023), so long-term track record is limited. While non-QM and specialty programs are advertised, specific rates, fees, and approval criteria are not disclosed on the website, requiring direct contact for accurate pricing comparison.
pros: Correspondent lender controls own pricing and underwriting—no broker middlemen or margin stacking
80+ investor relationships provide access to non-QM, bank statement, ITIN, asset depletion, and specialty programs most lenders cannot offer
Proprietary technology enables 24/7 loan officer availability and remote work, supporting fast turnarounds
DSCR investment loans allow qualification on rental income rather than personal income for real estate investors
Specialized programs for non-traditional borrowers (self-employed, freelancers, foreign nationals) without penalizing alternative income documentation
5.0 Google star rating (34 reviews) with consistent customer testimonials about fast closings and complex borrower handling
No stated middle management layer; loan officers directly control files
cons: Licensed in only 5 states (FL, CO, GA, TX, IL)—unavailable to borrowers in 45 states
Established only in 2023; no verifiable long-term performance history or portfolio quality data
Website does not disclose specific rates, fees, origination costs, or approval criteria—direct inquiry required for pricing
No mention of APR ranges, loan terms, or comparison pricing vs. traditional lenders
Limited independent third-party reviews or regulatory complaint data available to verify customer satisfaction claims
best_for: Self-employed and freelance borrowers needing bank statement or asset depletion loan documentation in licensed states
Real estate investors seeking DSCR investment loans or fix-and-flip financing
Borrowers with non-traditional credit or income situations (foreign nationals, ITIN borrowers, complex income) rejected by traditional lenders
Borrowers in FL, CO, GA, TX, or IL seeking fast mortgage closings with personalized loan officer service
diagnosis: Olympus Financial is best suited for self-employed borrowers, real estate investors, and non-traditional income earners in Florida, Colorado, Georgia, Texas, or Illinois seeking faster closings and access to non-QM programs unavailable at traditional lenders. Primary caveat: the company is geographically limited to 5 states, newly established (2023), and does not publicly disclose rates or fees, requiring direct contact to evaluate actual loan costs vs. alternatives.
services: FHA loans with low down payment options
VA loans for veterans and active military
Non-QM and alternative documentation loans (bank statement, asset depletion, ITIN, no-doc)
DSCR investment property loans (SFR, multi-family, short-term rentals)
Foreign national and specialty financing (non-warrantable condos, CondoFlex, hotel-classified)
